change_comments.message:
As discussed , since the auto detection policy is enabled by default so we might not need any extra changes to enable the members in the docker container to talk to each other.
Halil to verify this and change the patch accordingly.

waiting a confirmation from ETH team need to confirm on this page:

https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS-391Spike%3A+Define+and+Agree+NCMP+REST+Interface#CPS391Spike:DefineandAgreeNCMPRESTInterface-Requirements

inline_comments.message:
u removed this coz of 2 replicas now ? and 2 containers cant have the same name.
now we are relying on the auto-generated container names ???

Right. it is not possible to give a name to the container if the replica is 2.

In this case, container names will be like this (auto assigned):

docker-compose_cps-and-ncmp_1
docker-compose_cps-and-ncmp_2
NB: HAZELCAST_DOCKER_ENABLED

We can replace it with more proper way like below :

1. Add bom into parent cps

<dependencyManagement>
    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.hazelcast</groupId>
            <artifactId>hazelcast-bom</artifactId>
            <version>5.3.1</version>
            <type>pom</type>
            <scope>import</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>
</dependencyManagement>

2. Use here like this:
<dependencies>
    <!-- Hazelcast Core -->
    <dependency>
        <groupId>com.hazelcast</groupId>
        <artifactId>hazelcast</artifactId>
    </dependency>
    
    <!-- Hazelcast Spring Integration -->
    <dependency>
        <groupId>com.hazelcast</groupId>
        <artifactId>hazelcast-spring</artifactId>
    </dependency>
</dependencies>
Is there any specific reason to downgrade it as 5.3.1 is a newer major release of Hazelcast, indicating potential new features, improvements, and changes from the 4.x series?

Use random ids :

If we assign 1L to classes, the serialization mechanism cannot distinguish between different versions of classes.
This can cause deserialization to fail if there are changes in the class definition that are not compatible with the serialized object.

Use random ids :

If we assign 1L to classes, the serialization mechanism cannot distinguish between different versions of classes.
This can cause deserialization to fail if there are changes in the class definition that are not compatible with the serialized object.
Make use of bom version as into pre. comment

Is this word "cache" relevant here? as this property enables:

1. Efficiently discover and communicate with other Hazelcast instances running in different containers.
2. Integrate with Docker's orchestration tools, like Docker Compose and Kubernetes, for scaling and managing instances.
Is it correct level or should be only for debug?
By specifying replicas: 2, Docker Compose will start 2 instances of cps-and-ncmp, each with a unique container name (cps-and-ncmp_1, cps-and-ncmp_2, etc.).

There is no common version 5.3.1 for both artifacts: hazelcast-spring and hazelcast-all. Thus, bom would not work.

Also, HazelcastCacheConfig.java line 101:
config.getNetworkConfig().getJoin().getAutoDetectionConfig().setEnabled(true);

The method "getAutoDetectionConfig" that make Hazelcast members discover each other available "ONLY" in artifact(hazelcast-all) with the latest version: 4.2.8 See the links below:

https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/com.hazelcast/hazelcast-all

https://docs.hazelcast.com/imdg/4.2/installation/installing-using-maven


Plus, artifact: hazelcast-all provide the same functionality obtained by the artifact: hazelcast-spring. So no need to have two different artifacts being included in the project.
Please see the reasons mentioned above.
Done
Done
Answered above
Can you please suggest a good name?
info is fine since this logs not being printed many times but ll be printed while the containers initializing.

I kept it info level based on my comment above, since the WARN level may be used in case of any unexpected happened in the application.
 
 log.info("Neither Kubernetes nor Docker discovery mode is enabled");
Ack
Since the container names being assigned dynamicall, a single docker service name is sufficient here.
Done

1. Method com.hazelcast.config.JoinConfig#getAutoDetectionConfig is available into hazelcast version 5.3.1 (also into latest version 5.3.6) 

Ref: 
https://javadoc.io/static/com.hazelcast/hazelcast/5.3.1/com/hazelcast/client/config/ClientNetworkConfig.html#getAutoDetectionConfig--

https://javadoc.io/static/com.hazelcast/hazelcast/5.3.6/com/hazelcast/client/config/ClientNetworkConfig.html#getAutoDetectionConfig--

2. Advantage of using hazelcast-spring

Using hazelcast-spring provides a streamlined and efficient way to integrate Hazelcast into Spring applications, leveraging the powerful features of both Hazelcast and the Spring framework. This leads to more maintainable, flexible, and scalable applications. like : org.onap.cps.ncmp.impl.inventory.sync.SynchronizationCacheConfig, org.onap.cps.cache.AnchorDataCacheConfig#anchorDataCache etc

3. Use hazelcast-spring:5.3.1 or 5.3.6
Use hazelcast-spring:5.3.1 or 5.3.6 as suggested above.

Use hazelcast-spring: 

1.Overhead and Size: The hazelcast-all JAR includes all Hazelcast modules, which can increase the size of your application's footprint unnecessarily if you are only using a subset of Hazelcast features. 

2. Integration Complexity: hazelcast-all is a single JAR file that includs unnecessary modules.

3. Spring Integration: hazelcast-spring, on the other hand, provides Spring-specific integration features and configurations that makes it easier to integrate Hazelcast with Spring beans, manage lifecycle etc.

I think kubernetesDiscoveryEnabled and dockerDiscoveryEnabled would be more suitable names that reflect their purpose. Since these variables are used to enable or disable specific Hazelcast discovery modes. 

Still we can take teams suggestion
